defs: The act of one who calls; a crying aloud, esp. in order to summon, or to attract the attention of, some one.
A summoning or convocation, as of Parliament.
quotes: The frequent calling and meeting of Parlaiment.

defs: A divine summons or invitation; also, the state of being divinely called.
quotes: Who hath . . . called us with an holy calling.
Give diligence to make yior calling . . . sure.

defs: A naming, or inviting; a reading over or reciting in order, or a call of names with a view to obtaining an answer, as in legislative bodies.
One's usual occupation, or employment; vocation; business; trade.
quotes: The humble calling of ter female parent.

defs: The persons, collectively, engaged in any particular professions or employment.
quotes: To impose celibacy on wholy callings.
I am more proud to be Sir Rowland's son His youngest son, and would not change that calling.
